_comment: Baselines for the CFG/ILP robustness suite (tests/cfg/*.c), run by tests/cfg_test.py. Each entry pins the observed outcome of one control-flow shape through the MSP430 toolchain. 'expected: <int>' pins a WCET; 'expected: null' records a documented gap that produces no WCET. See each .c file's header comment and the findings in the CFG/ILP robustness plan.

multicall.note: Several calls in one source block: CallSplitterPass splits the block at each call and ProgramGraph wires every call/return pair.

multiret.note: Callee with multiple return blocks; each isReturnBlock() is wired back to the caller's landing block.

switchcase.note: Switch dispatch (jump table / branch chain): multi-successor blocks and a merge point.

selfloop.note: Single pragma-bounded loop (volatile accumulator prevents closed-form folding); loop-bound row emitted, WCET finite.

nestedloops.note: Nested loop plus a sibling loop, all pragma-bounded; loop-bound aggregation across nesting and multiple headers.

noreturn.note: Call to a noreturn callee (exit): no return edge wired (HasLanding == false). exit is a body-less external callee, so the WCET is flagged UNSOUND but still produced.

trivial.note: Minimal start function: single block, no loops or calls.

indirect.note: GAP (finding #3): genuine indirect (function-pointer) call via a volatile-indexed table. The callee is NOT wired into the graph and its cost is omitted -- a silent under-approximation that is NOT flagged unsound. Pinned at the observed value; when indirect calls are modeled this will change (YELLOW -> refresh).

recursion_simple.note: Self-recursion (fib) bounded via #pragma recursion_bound(20). finalize() treats fib's entry as a loop header whose backedges are the two recursive call edges (fib(x-1), fib(x-2)), capping total invocations at 20, so the IPET loop-bound row yields a finite WCET. Was finding #5's documented gap; now closed.

recursion_linear.note: Linear single-call-site self-recursion (sum) bounded via #pragma recursion_bound(11): the recursive call edge is the single backedge into sum's entry, capped at 11 total invocations. Finite WCET.

recursion_unbounded.note: Self-recursion (fac) with NO recursion_bound: the recursive call edge forms an unbounded cycle, so NO WCET is produced (solver: infeasible-or-unbounded). finalize() reports 'RECURSION: ... has no recursion_bound; ... UNBOUNDED'. Turns YELLOW if unbounded self-recursion ever starts producing a WCET (which would be unsound).

mutual_recursion.note: Mutual recursion (ping<->pong), a 2-function call-graph cycle bounded via #pragma recursion_bound. main calls ping(n) (header=ping); a DFS over the SCC's call edges marks pong->ping as the backedge into ping's entry, capped at ping's bound (pong bounded transitively) -> finite WCET. Was expect-unbounded before mutual recursion was bounded.

mutual_recursion_anka.note: The Maelardalen anka/kalle pattern, made reachable (main calls kalle(n)). Header=kalle; anka->kalle is the cycle backedge. Verifies the same pattern that is dead code in srcMaelardalen/recursion.c now bounds when on the analysis path.

mutual_recursion3.note: Three-function cycle a->b->c->a bounded via #pragma recursion_bound. main calls a(n) (header=a); the DFS finds c->a as the backedge. Verifies back-edge detection beyond a 2-node cycle.

mutual_recursion_loop.note: Mutual recursion (ping<->pong) invoked from a pragma-bounded loop in main. The header bound multiplies by the loop's external-entry count (IPET row caps header at B*non-backedge-inflow), which a flat x<=B cap would get wrong. Verifies external-entry scaling.

mutual_recursion_unbounded.note: Negative case: 2-function cycle up<->down where the header up() (called from main) has NO recursion_bound, so the cycle is UNBOUNDED and NO WCET is produced -- a non-header bound on down() does not rescue it. finalize() reports 'RECURSION: mutual recursion among {down, up} is UNBOUNDED'. Guards the safety boundary.

shiftloop.note: Multi-bit shift on a wide type. MSP430 has no barrel shifter, so the backend synthesizes a shift loop with no source pragma and no IR loop; the implicit loop-bound path (RTTarget::getImplicitLoopBound) bounds it. Same category that previously left compress/edn unbounded.

gotoloop.note: Loop formed by a backward goto rather than for/while. Exercises the LoopBoundPlugin's backward-goto recognition (pragma JSON emitted) and the bound's application to the resulting machine loop.
